04326.content: PHive must keep domain models persistence-agnostic while still enabling rich storage behavior. Models declare persistence intent through annotations such as PHiveType and PHiveField, and generated PTypeAdapter implementations own hook orchestration, payload serialization, metadata handling, and Hive integration. Core runtime and generator behavior are developed under a TDD-first workflow.

05abd.summary: Expose hook-driven persistence without wrapper models
05abd.content: Standard Hive CE is fast but lacks first-class field-level and model-level interception for behaviors such as encryption, validation, and TTL. PHive exists to provide those behaviors without leaking persistence wrappers into application models. The intended developer experience is generator-first: users write normal Dart or Freezed models, declare hooks statically in annotations, and let generated adapters handle context propagation and hook execution transparently.
